project: SICOT – Système Intégré de Coopération Internationale et de Traduction
currentSprintFocus: Sprint 11 (Analytics & Rapports, M11) fully COMPLÉTÉ per docs/TASKS.md, plus an unplanned post-closure add-on this session: AI-generated report narratives via Gemini (modules/analytics/services/gemini*.ts, modules/report rapports IA workflow), with a mandatory human-review gate before any AI text is considered final. ⚠️ package-lock.json shifted this session — exceljs downgraded ^4.4.0→^3.4.0 (used by all 3 Excel export features built across Sprint 10/11), node-cron/nodemailer/uuid all jumped multiple majors — looks like an accidental root-level `npm install`, not yet re-verified.
status: Sprints 1–5 + 8 + 9 + 10 + 11 COMPLETE ✅ per docs/TASKS.md (7a1de70, 47ef8b8, 6aaa354, f3547d4+f27d58f+Gemini add-on). Server refactor pass COMPLETE ✅ (dd2809d). Sprint 11 now includes an AI report-narrative feature (Gemini) with admin review workflow. None of Sprints 9/10/11 has had a full manual end-to-end verification pass yet, and this session's dependency version changes (esp. exceljs downgrade) haven't been re-tested either.

activeSession.task: Sprint 11 post-closure add-on — Rapports IA (Gemini): every generated rapport can now get an AI-written Markdown narrative (French, 'Résumé exécutif/Tendances/Points d'attention/Recommandations'), generated automatically by the monthly cron or on-demand from AnalyticsPage's Rapports tab, but never treated as final until an admin reviews and validates it. New `modules/analytics/services/gemini.service.ts` (anonymizes agent names before any Gemini call — mandatory, not policy-gated; deterministic deltas computed in code vs. the last *validated* report, never by the model; hard activity floor below which no API call is made at all; 3-model rotation with a self-imposed daily quota per model, well under the real free-tier limit; thinking budget pinned to near-zero) + `gemini-quota.service.ts` (two independent daily caps: per-model call count, and a global cap on on-demand-triggered generations only — the cron's automatic runs don't count). New `rapports` columns (`contenuIA` raw / `contenuIAValide` frozen-after-review / `statutRelectureIA` state machine / `moteurIA` pinned model name / `relecteurIAId` / `relusLeIA`), new `geminiUsageQuotidien` + `rapportsIAQuotidien` tables. Client: review dialog on the Rapports tab using `react-markdown` (deliberately not dangerouslySetInnerHTML, since the content is untrusted LLM output) with validate/reject (confirmation required — a bug found in testing), regenerate-after-reject; new Gemini usage monitor section in `AdminParametresPage.tsx` (per-model bars, thinking-token counts, auto-refresh 60s). Real bugs found & fixed in the process (see docs/TASKS.md + sessions/2026-07-04.md for detail): `listerRapports()` omitted the IA fields entirely (every row showed 'IA - rejeté' regardless of true status), rapports were sorted oldest-first, `cn()` lacked `tailwind-merge` (custom className didn't override component defaults — fixed at the root, may fix the same bug elsewhere in the app), and DocumentsPage.tsx had its own unsynced category list (rapport documents showed a blank category). docs/TASKS.md Sprint 11 header is now ✅ COMPLÉTÉ with extensive documentation of this whole add-on. NOT in TASKS.md: this session's package-lock.json changed ~2800 lines — exceljs downgraded from ^4.4.0 to ^3.4.0 (confirmed installed), the exact library 3 different Excel export features (Sprint 10 audit, Sprint 11 analytics, Sprint 11 rapports) depend on and were built/tested against 4.x; node-cron/nodemailer/uuid also jumped multiple majors. Root package.json also picked up duplicate direct deps on these same packages. Pattern strongly suggests an `npm install` run from the repo root instead of inside packages/server.
activeSession.nextAction: Priority: restore `exceljs` to `^4.4.0` in packages/server/package.json (and root package.json — remove the duplicated exceljs/node-cron/nodemailer/uuid entries there entirely, they don't belong at the root) and re-run `npm install`, then manually re-test all 3 Excel exports (audit journal, analytics dashboard, rapports) since they were validated against exceljs 4.x. Also manually smoke-test node-cron jobs and nodemailer email sending given the major version jumps. Then: run `tsc --noEmit` both packages, manually walk the Gemini rapports IA flow end-to-end (generate report → generate AI analysis → review dialog → validate → confirm frozen text persists → check quota monitor updates), delete/fix the still-dead `modules/report/routes/rapports.route.ts` empty stub, generate one Drizzle migration covering everything hand-edited into schema.ts across Sprint 9/10/11 (portailTokens, documents columns, courriersCriticiteSnapshots, rapports table + new IA columns, rapportTypeEnum/rapportFormatEnum/statutRelectureIAEnum, geminiUsageQuotidien, rapportsIAQuotidien, documentCategorieEnum's 'rapport' value). Still open from Sprint 9: fix `/portail` → `/portal` href bug in DocumentsPage.tsx. Still open from Sprint 10: manual verification of parameter-driven OTP/lockout/backup-retention/deepl behaviors.

criticalRules: CHECK exceljs version in packages/server/package.json is ^4.4.0 before trusting any Excel export (audit/analytics/rapports) — accidentally downgraded to ^3.4.0 on 2026-07-04, likely via a root-level npm install; not yet re-verified against 3.x API
NEVER run shadcn CLI — Tailwind v4 incompatibility
NEVER put transition inside framer-motion Variants objects
NEVER use CORS origin wildcard with credentials: true
ALWAYS use @/ path alias for client imports
ALWAYS use ANAC design tokens (bg-anac-navy), never raw hex
